findings.summary: the record for the previous sha marked m5 'fixed' while the fix lands in THIS commit — a per-sha attestation describing a state its own sha did not have. Corrected to 'open' there; this record covers the fix
the previous sha's review record rides along in this commit alongside an unrelated code fix — a governance artifact bundled with a behaviour change, defensible but a separate concern

ruledOut: pattern correctness: .vfkb/.write-probe-* matches what src/doctor.ts:555 builds (pid+random), verified live with git check-ignore
over-match: entries.jsonl and manifest.json remain un-ignored; `git ls-files | git check-ignore --stdin` is empty, so nothing tracked becomes hidden
generator half: the entry is in src/init.ts's emitted list and the tests assert the EMITTED file via real git check-ignore in a temp repo, not a source grep; deleting the line turns both red
consistency: vfkb's own .gitignore and init.ts's emitted stanza now carry the same six .vfkb/* paths in the same order — no remaining drift (the #219 class)
protocols: no AI attribution, no secrets
